summary: MDM4U counting problems where order matters use permutations: the number of ordered arrangements of r objects from n is nPr = n!/(n−r)!. Think seating, finishing order, or forming codes.
formulas: arrange all n distinct objects: n!
method: Decide whether order matters — if it does, use permutations.
Identify n (objects available) and r (positions to fill).
If every object is placed, the count is just n!.
Watch for restrictions (repeats, fixed positions) and adjust the count.

example.title: Arrange 3 of 5 books on a shelf
steps: Order matters, so use permutations with n = 5, r = 3.
